title: maxillaria foetida
intro: Maxillaria foetida, also known as the stink orchid, is a species of orchid that gets its name from its distinctive and pungent odor. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Maxillaria foetida are said to include the relief of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to create teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
